blurb: Stewards B.C.'s forest and range lands — timber tenures and pricing, wildfire management through the BC Wildfire Service, and forest health and silviculture.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: B.C.'s forests are stewarded sustainably for future generations
goals.description: The ministry maintains healthy, resilient forests through reforestation, forest health monitoring, old growth protection and collaborative landscape planning with First Nations and communities.

main_services.name: Reforest and monitor forest health after harvesting

supporting.name: Report silviculture surveys and reforestation data
supporting.desc: Licensees and surveyors submit stocking surveys and reforestation results through the RESULTS system to confirm that free-growing and reforestation requirements are being met.

channels.label: RESULTS - Reporting Silviculture Updates and Land Status Tracking System

legislation.full_name: Forest and Range Practices Act  -  Stocking standards and reforestation obligations

supporting.name: Get guidance on forest pest, disease and wildfire-damaged stand management
supporting.desc: The ministry monitors bark beetles, defoliators, pathogens and other forest health factors through aerial overview surveys and publishes forest health bulletins and chief forester guidance for managing affected stands.

supporting.name: Access funding for forest health and silviculture innovation
supporting.desc: The Silviculture Innovation Program and related forest investment funding accelerate research and adoption of innovative reforestation and stand-tending practices.

main_services.name: Protect old growth forests and plan forest landscapes

supporting.name: Learn about old growth deferral areas and forest management
supporting.desc: Information on old growth strategic review recommendations, deferral areas and the province's action plan to change how ancient forests are managed.
supporting.users: General public, First Nations, industry, conservation groups

supporting.name: Participate in forest landscape planning with First Nations and communities
supporting.desc: Forest landscape plans bring together First Nations, government, industry and local representation to set long-term approaches to managing timber, cultural and biodiversity values.

main_services.name: Prepare and track a forest stewardship plan

supporting.name: Prepare a forest stewardship plan for your tenure
supporting.desc: Forest agreement holders must prepare and get approval for a forest stewardship plan under the Forest Act before harvesting or road-building can begin.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Requirement for an approved forest stewardship plan

supporting.name: Track your forest stewardship plan online
supporting.desc: The FSP Tracking System lets licensees and the public follow the status of forest stewardship plans through the review and approval process.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Crown timber is allocated, priced and harvested responsibly
goals.description: The ministry administers forest tenures, timber sales, pricing and scaling so that Crown timber is harvested, measured and paid for fairly, from large commercial licences down to a household firewood permit.

main_services.name: Get rights to harvest Crown timber

supporting.name: Apply for a woodlot licence
supporting.desc: A woodlot licence grants the holder exclusive rights to manage and harvest Crown timber within a small licence area, awarded competitively or by direct award to First Nations.
supporting.users: Small forest operators, individuals, First Nations

supporting.name: Apply for a community forest agreement
supporting.desc: A community forest agreement is an area-based licence managed by a local government, community group or First Nation for the benefit of the whole community, awarded by direct invitation.
supporting.users: Local governments, community groups, First Nations

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 7.1: Community forest agreements

supporting.name: Get a licence to cut timber on Crown land
supporting.desc: Forestry licences to cut and occupant licences to cut authorize one-time or occupant-based timber harvesting outside of a major tenure, including for wildfire salvage or land occupants.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 8.2: Licences to cut

supporting.name: Get a road permit to build a forestry resource road
supporting.desc: A road permit authorizes construction, modification and use of a resource road to access Crown timber, and may include the right to harvest timber within the right-of-way.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Part 8: Roads and rights of way

main_services.name: Get a permit for personal timber use on Crown land

supporting.name: Get a free-use permit for firewood
supporting.desc: Members of the public can get a free 'Free Use Permit for Firewood' from a Natural Resource District to cut dead or downed trees on vacant Crown land for personal use only.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 9: Free use permits for First Nations and others

supporting.name: Get a permit to cut a Christmas tree on Crown land
supporting.desc: Members of the public can get a free 'Free Use Permit for a Christmas Tree' from each Natural Resource District where they intend to cut one tree for personal use.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 9.1: Christmas tree permits

supporting.name: Get a free use permit for cultural, scientific or agricultural timber use
supporting.desc: Free use permits authorize removal of minor volumes of Crown timber for First Nation traditional and cultural activities, scientific investigation, mining claim development or agricultural lease purposes.
supporting.users: First Nations, researchers, agricultural lease holders

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 9: Free use permits for First Nations and others

main_services.name: Register, mark and transport harvested timber

supporting.name: Apply for a private timber mark
supporting.desc: A private timber mark certifies ownership and is required to transport logs harvested from privately-owned land, helping prevent theft of Crown timber.

supporting.name: Apply for a forest client number
supporting.desc: A forest client number is required to do business with the Ministry of Forests — receiving volume statements, invoices, payments, contracts and official correspondence.

supporting.name: Apply to export logs from British Columbia
supporting.desc: Exporting unmanufactured timber requires an exemption from the requirement to manufacture in B.C., a provincial export permit and, for exports outside Canada, a federal permit.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Part 10: Manufacture in British Columbia

main_services.name: Get timber priced, measured and sold

supporting.name: Look up stumpage rates for Crown timber
supporting.desc: Stumpage is the fee paid to government for timber harvested from Crown land; rates are set through coast and interior appraisal systems and published for licensees.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Part 7: Payments to the government (stumpage)

supporting.name: Submit harvest billing and scale data
supporting.desc: The Harvest Billing System manages scale data submission and invoicing for stumpage owed on timber harvested from Crown land.

supporting.name: Bid on a BC Timber Sales timber sale licence
supporting.desc: BC Timber Sales auctions up to 13 million cubic metres of public timber annually through competitive timber sale licences, setting cost and price benchmarks for the province.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Division 3: Timber sale licences

supporting.name: Bid on BC Timber Sales contracting opportunities
supporting.desc: BCTS advertises contracting opportunities in planning, development, professional services and silviculture across its 33 business areas and field offices.

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: British Columbians stay safe from wildfire and burn responsibly
goals.description: The BC Wildfire Service prevents, prepares for, responds to and recovers from wildfire, and regulates campfires and open burning to keep people and communities safe.

main_services.name: Prevent wildfires and follow fire restrictions

supporting.name: Check current campfire bans and fire restrictions
supporting.desc: Check open fire prohibitions, equipment/activity prohibitions and area restrictions by fire centre before lighting a campfire or open fire anywhere in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Wildfire Act  -  ss.10-13: Prohibition, restriction and leave area orders

relationships.service: Get a permit to conduct open burning

supporting.name: Register a Category 3 open fire
supporting.desc: Anyone planning a category 3 open fire (large piles, windrows, grass or stubble burning) must obtain a burn registration number through the Open Fire Tracking System before burning.

legislation.full_name: Wildfire Act  -  Wildfire Regulation, s.22: Category 3 open fire requirements

supporting.name: Apply for wildfire risk reduction funding for your community
supporting.desc: The Community Resiliency Investment program and the Forest Enhancement Society of BC fund FireSmart activities and fuel management projects on Crown and private land.

supporting.name: Report a wildfire
supporting.desc: Call 1-800-663-5555 or *5555 from a cell phone to report a wildfire to the BC Wildfire Service.

channels.label: Report a wildfire: 1-800-663-5555 (*5555 on a cell)

supporting.name: Check the current wildfire situation and evacuation alerts
supporting.desc: View an interactive map of active wildfires, wildfire statistics and provincial evacuation orders and alerts.
supporting.users: General public, residents in fire-affected areas

relationships.service: Get verified emergency information during an active disaster

supporting.name: Get post-wildfire timber salvage guidance
supporting.desc: Wildfire timber salvage guidance sets planning and cutting permit requirements so tenure holders and non-tenure holders can recover fire-damaged timber and reforest affected areas.

channels.label: Wildfire Timber Salvage Planning and Administration Guide

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  Cutting permits for wildfire timber salvage

main_services.name: Work safely in forestry and timber sale operations

supporting.name: Get SAFE Company safety certification for forestry operations
supporting.desc: Companies and contractors working on BC Timber Sales licence areas must be SAFE Company certified, endorsed as new entrants, or otherwise safety certified through the BC Forest Safety Council.

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Forest workers, businesses and communities adapt to a changing sector
goals.description: As the forest sector faces mill closures, softwood lumber tariffs and reduced harvest levels, the ministry supports workers, contractors and communities and helps grow higher-value wood manufacturing and trade.

main_services.name: Get support through forestry sector change

supporting.name: Get retraining and job search support as a forestry worker
supporting.desc: WorkBC Centres, the StrongerBC future skills grant and SkilledTradesBC pre-apprenticeship programs help forestry workers affected by curtailments and closures find new jobs and training.

channels.label: Supports for Forestry Workers, Communities and Businesses

supporting.name: Apply for manufacturing modernization funding for your forestry business
supporting.desc: The BC Manufacturing Jobs Fund helps forestry manufacturers modernize, innovate and grow through funding for capital projects, prioritizing retrofits to new value-added business lines.

supporting.name: Get community transition support after a mill closure
supporting.desc: Community Transition Services coordinate on-the-ground supports across ministries and organizations for individuals and communities affected by a mill closure or other economic disruption.

main_services.name: Grow value-added wood manufacturing and trade

supporting.name: Get support to diversify into value-added wood manufacturing
supporting.desc: Value-Added Accelerators help wood product manufacturers sustain, diversify and grow their operations to create more jobs from the same volume of harvested timber.

supporting.name: Get information on softwood lumber trade issues with the U.S.
supporting.desc: Information on the U.S. lumber industry's softwood tariff petitions and other cross-border trade issues affecting Canadian lumber shipments.

channels.label: Softwood Lumber Dispute with the U.S.

foundational_services.name: Determine allowable annual cut for timber supply areas and tree farm licences
foundational_services.desc: The chief forester, an independent statutory decision maker, determines allowable annual cut levels for Crown forest land based on environmental, social and economic factors.

legislation.full_name: Forest Act  -  s.8: Allowable annual cut

foundational_services.name: Maintain forest resource inventory and timber supply data
foundational_services.desc: Ongoing collection and maintenance of forest inventory, timber supply and forest health data used to inform allowable annual cut and stewardship decisions.

foundational_services.name: Administer forest tenure files and cut control
foundational_services.desc: Internal administration of forest tenure records, cut control accounting and compliance monitoring across all forms of forest agreement.
